title: vicia ludoviciana var. typica
intro: Vicia ludoviciana var. typica, commonly known as Ludwig's vetch or Ludwig's vetch pea, is a perennial legume plant native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and is used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Vicia ludoviciana var. typica include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
